title: Trivia

jokes.text: You really have to say 'oh yeah,' every time you eat a candy bar? / I can't help it, Oscar. It's just really good. / Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Oscar calls out Kevin's automatic candy bar reaction
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately does it again while explaining he can't help it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin character moment - his complete lack of self-awareness about his compulsive behavior

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Kevin, you automatically reach for a candy bar every time someone mentions food.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: No I don't.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Yes you do. It's like Pavlov's dog.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: *automatically reaches for a candy bar*

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I can't help it, man. It just happens.

jokes.text: It was a raccoon eating a hamburger like a person!
jokes.setup: Andy asks them to get distractions out before the meeting
jokes.punchline: Erin's bizarre, childlike excitement over mundane animal behavior
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Erin - her innocent wonder at absurd things, delivered with perfect specificity

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! Did you guys know that birds can see in ultraviolet? That means they see a completely different world than us. A magical world.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: And squirrels can rotate their tails to help them balance. How is that not the coolest thing you've ever heard?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Also, I read that otters hold hands when they sleep so they don't drift apart. That is so sweet I might actually die.

jokes.text: ♪ I'ma love you downstairs tonight ♪
jokes.setup: Andy trying to run a professional meeting
jokes.punchline: Creed interrupting with inappropriate sexual song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed inappropriate interjection - stays true to character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone, let's keep this meeting focused and professional.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: She had dumps like a truck, truck, truck, trucks like a dump, dump, dump.

jokes.text: You can write a book about chairs.
jokes.setup: Andy asks what can be done with paper
jokes.punchline: Kevin's oddly specific and pointless suggestion
jokes.explanation: Kevin's simple mind providing an unexpectedly specific but useless answer

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: You could use it to wrap up a wet burrito so the juices don't get all over your hands.

jokes.text: There is an infinity of things that you can do with paper!
jokes.setup: Andy trying to lead an inspiring sales meeting
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top dramatic delivery of obvious information
jokes.explanation: Andy's tendency to oversell basic concepts with misplaced enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: People of Dunder Mifflin! Today, we gather not merely as salespeople, but as WARRIORS of commerce!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And what is the secret to our success? I'll tell you: we must... sell... paper.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's right. Paper. The very substance upon which civilization itself is written!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So go forth, my friends, and sell it with the passion of a thousand suns!

jokes.text: Let's get high on our own supply!
jokes.setup: Andy wants employees to buy their own company's paper
jokes.punchline: Using drug dealer terminology for office supplies
jokes.explanation: Clever appropriation of criminal terminology for mundane business practice

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, so I need you all to buy our paper. Think of it like this: I'm your supplier, you're my customers, and this is a transaction.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You come to me, on the day of my daughter's wedding, and you ask me for twenty reams? That's a big ask.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: But I got you. I'm good for it. I hook you up with the primo stuff. The good stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Word of mouth spreads. Pretty soon, everyone wants Andy's paper. You're all fiending for it.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's when I raise the price. Supply and demand, people.

jokes.text: And I can't afford to keep buying paper from us.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining the sales shortfall
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's been personally buying company products out of desperation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment revealing Andy's incompetence and desperation as manager

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Look, I know the sales numbers are down, and I take full responsibility for that.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: But what you don't know is that I've been personally buying our products out of desperation just to keep the numbers from looking even worse.

jokes.text: I need to sell the $2,200 of paper that's in my garage.
jokes.setup: Previous admission about buying company paper
jokes.punchline: Revealing the absurd extent of his personal paper stockpile
jokes.explanation: Escalation that makes Andy's situation even more pathetic and absurd

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I may have gone a little overboard with the personal paper stockpile.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I have 47 reams in my apartment.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And another 23 in my car.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Plus, I rented a storage unit. It's just... all paper.

jokes.text: Animals, machines, vast virtual armies. All of these things I have successfully managed. The only thing I haven't managed, is people.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his management experience in talking head
jokes.punchline: Listing increasingly bizarre things while admitting he can't manage people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight delusion - claiming expertise in impossible things while missing the obvious

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have extensive management experience. I've managed beet farms, paper salesmen, and a volunteer sheriff's deputy force. I've managed my subordinates through layoffs, office romances, and a fire in the building. I've managed a warehouse, a branch, and at one point, a small militia. But I'll be honest—I cannot manage people.

jokes.text: So, like a Spanish conquistador, I have come to Florida to claim what is rightfully mine.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing his job interview trip
jokes.punchline: Comparing a corporate meeting to violent colonial conquest
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Dwight grandiosity - turning mundane situations into epic historical parallels

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: The meeting was like a violent colonial conquest. I decimated them with facts and figures. By the end, their stock price had surrendered unconditionally.

jokes.text: I'm not here to be given anything. I'm here to take what is mine.
jokes.setup: Receptionist offering to get Dwight something
jokes.punchline: Dwight's unnecessarily aggressive response to simple hospitality
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inability to interact normally, treating office visits like military operations

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Can I get you anything?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No. And if you try to poison me, I will hurt you.

jokes.text: Surrounded by shrubbery, like a squirrel's office.
jokes.setup: Dwight commenting on Gabe's office
jokes.punchline: Bizarre animal comparison for standard office landscaping
jokes.explanation: Dwight's unique way of processing normal environments through animal metaphors

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Gabe's office is like a lair. It's like he's a supervillain or something. The way he's got those plants in there, it's like he's got a pet tarantula or a piranha that he's feeding.

jokes.text: I can think of no better way to confront my deathly fear of flying.
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining his frequent travel between offices
jokes.punchline: Claiming exposure therapy while clearly suffering from it
jokes.explanation: Gabe's masochistic approach to problem-solving, delivered with his typical awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I travel between offices frequently as a form of exposure therapy. It helps me confront my fear of flying.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I'm getting better at it every day.

jokes.text: I don't know what's worse, the trip or the destination.
jokes.setup: Robert commenting on Dwight's initiative in coming to Florida
jokes.punchline: Insulting both Dwight's effort and Florida itself
jokes.explanation: Robert's casual cruelty delivered with sophisticated indifference

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: Dwight, I have to say, I'm impressed. You flew all the way down here to Florida. That takes initiative. That takes guts.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: Of course, so does moving to Florida, and look how that turned out for the state.

jokes.text: So make that error! / I can make that error.
jokes.setup: Andy asking Kevin to fudge the numbers as a 'rounding error'
jokes.punchline: Kevin enthusiastically volunteering to make mistakes
jokes.explanation: Kevin's eagerness to do what he's naturally good at - making errors

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Kevin, I need you to fudge the numbers a little bit. Just call it a rounding error.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Oh, I can do that. I make mistakes all the time. I'm really good at it.

jokes.text: I know that making errors sounds like your kind of thing, but it's a little more complicated than that.
jokes.setup: Kevin offering to help with accounting
jokes.punchline: Oscar's polite but cutting assessment of Kevin's abilities
jokes.explanation: Oscar's diplomatic way of insulting Kevin while maintaining professional demeanor

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Kevin, I appreciate the offer, but I need someone who can actually do accounting.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I can do accounting!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Kevin, you once asked me what a deduction was.

jokes.text: Triviocalypse!
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining why he can't stay late
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top name for a trivia contest
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that reveals Oscar's surprising nerdy enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: I can't stay late. I have to get home for the International Symposium of Advanced Categorical Phenomonology and Metataxonomy.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's just trivia night at Chili's.

jokes.text: Gabe, I always wondered what it is you do around here. Now I found out. You're the bagel guy.
jokes.setup: Gabe negotiating with bagel vendor
jokes.punchline: Jim reducing Gabe's entire job to bagel procurement
jokes.explanation: Jim's dry observation cutting through corporate pretense to harsh reality

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So you're saying your entire job is just about getting the bagels?

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: No, that's not what I'm saying at all. I have many responsibilities as the corporate compliance officer.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Right, right. But today, this moment, what you're doing... that's bagels.

jokes.text: Question, what's the most important appliance in your house? / Meat grinder. / Too slow. It's the toilet.
jokes.setup: Gabe asking rhetorical question to set up metaphor
jokes.punchline: Dwight's immediate wrong answer reveals his priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Dwight's meat grinder answer is quintessentially him

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: What's the difference between a shoe and a telephone?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: One is a shoe and one is a telephone.

jokes.text: I am the toilet of this office. I flush away annoying problems so others can keep their hands clean, and, just like a toilet, I am essential.
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining his role metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Proudly comparing himself to a toilet with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Peak Gabe - earnestly degrading himself while thinking he's being profound

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I'm like a toilet. I take everyone's shit.

jokes.text: You could have gone with garbage disposal, incinerator, or eraser, and instead, you chose toilet. God bless you. You're an American classic.
jokes.setup: Gabe's toilet metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jim pointing out how Gabe chose the worst possible comparison
jokes.explanation: Jim's perfect deadpan response highlighting Gabe's poor judgment with fake reverence

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: Life is like a toilet. You sit on it, you do your business, you flush it away.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's... that's the worst metaphor I've ever heard.

jokes.text: CEO to COO, what a difference a letter makes.
jokes.setup: Dwight being told he'll meet with COO instead of CEO
jokes.punchline: Dwight's disappointed but philosophical observation
jokes.explanation: Dwight trying to find wisdom in disappointment, decent wordplay but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: COO? Not CEO? Well, that's disappointing. But I suppose the COO is still a respectable position. Second in command. Like a vice president, but with more authority. I can work with that. In fact, I've always believed that the true power in any organization lies not at the top, but one rung below. That's where the real decisions are made. So really, I'm not disappointed at all. This is actually perfect.

jokes.text: this is a gay bar. / Wait, what? / Everyone in here is gay? / Yes! It's a gay bar!
jokes.setup: The team arrives at trivia venue
jokes.punchline: Andy's obliviousness to obvious contextual clues
jokes.explanation: Andy's sheltered cluelessness creates awkward situation but relatively predictable

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Guys, I am so pumped for trivia night! I have been studying all week.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Andy, this is a bar trivia night. You don't study for this.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh please, Darryl. I've got this locked down. I even made flash cards.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Of what? Things that happened in 1987?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: The year I was born. Why, what's the connection?

jokes.text: Go on, kid. You know you don't belong here.
jokes.setup: Andy sorting team members by ability
jokes.punchline: Crushing dismissal delivered like tough love
jokes.explanation: Andy's misguided attempt at motivational leadership through humiliation

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, here's how I'm ranking you guys by ability. Kevin, you're at the bottom. Oscar, you're in the middle. And Jim, you're at the top.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: What? Why am I at the bottom?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Because you're the worst.

jokes.text: Let's reverse engineer this. You're a black singer, where do you go? Somewhere where you're a novelty. Alaska?
jokes.setup: Trivia question about Ray Charles and Georgia
jokes.punchline: Andy's spectacularly wrong and inappropriate reasoning
jokes.explanation: Andy's privileged obliviousness leading to accidentally offensive logic

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Ray Charles is from Georgia because he's blind and Georgia is a state you can really feel your way around.

jokes.text: How am I supposed to know what was on his mind? Oh, what do blind people think about? Okay, dogs, canes, signs, manholes, stairs, piano, darkness.
jokes.setup: Andy struggling with the Ray Charles question
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation leading to stereotypical assumptions
jokes.explanation: More cringe from Andy but verging on offensive territory without sufficient character payoff

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, Ray Charles was blind, right? So how did he... I mean, did he actually play the piano or did someone just move his hands around?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Andy, what are you talking about? Ray Charles was one of the greatest musicians of all time.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I know, I know. I'm just saying, if he couldn't see the keys, how did he know where to—

jokes.text: There're no fires within eight miles of here. / Well, it's nine miles away.
jokes.setup: Gabe claiming the COO had to fight a fire
jokes.punchline: Dwight's fact-check and Gabe's weak response
jokes.explanation: Dwight's literal fact-checking exposing Gabe's obvious lie

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A fire? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I don't know the specifics.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Well, I would have heard about it. I'm very involved in local emergency services.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: It was a small fire.

jokes.text: I can see you in the jungle now.
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to handle Dwight's frustration
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur that makes no contextual sense
jokes.explanation: Gabe's awkward attempt at defusing tension with random imagery

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: Bizarre non-sequitur that makes no contextual sense

jokes.text: Ladameon Washington.
jokes.setup: NBA trivia question about sixth man award winner
jokes.punchline: Andy's completely made-up player name delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Andy's fake name sounds plausible enough to be funny rather than just wrong

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: The answer is... Reggie Philbin.

jokes.text: Well, I know Elizabeth Taylor's sixth man was Richard Burton. Is that helpful?
jokes.setup: NBA sixth man question
jokes.punchline: Phyllis misunderstanding 'sixth man' as relationship terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection showing Phyllis's pop culture knowledge in unexpected context

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Wait, so the sixth man is like when you're dating someone but you're also seeing someone else on the side?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Because Bob would never do that to me.

jokes.text: If it'd been Ron Artest, it would have come up in Dancing With The Stars, when they pan over his trophy case when he's at home with his family.
jokes.setup: Discussion of NBA sixth man winner
jokes.punchline: Kelly's reality TV-based reasoning for sports knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly - filtering all knowledge through reality TV and celebrity culture

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I know all about the sixth man because I watch a lot of reality TV, and on reality TV, the sixth man is always the one who stirs up drama and causes all the problems.

jokes.text: Lamar winning sixth man was a big storyline on Chloe And Lamar.
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining how she knows sports facts
jokes.punchline: Getting NBA knowledge from Kardashian reality shows
jokes.explanation: Extension of Kelly's reality TV obsession providing unexpected sports expertise

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I know a lot about basketball because I watch the Kardashians, and they're always at the games. So I've learned a lot of NBA facts just from watching their show.

jokes.text: Ryan is MSNBC. I'm E!, TLC, and Oxygen.
jokes.setup: Andy assigning TV network identities to team members
jokes.punchline: Kelly claiming all the trashy entertainment networks
jokes.explanation: Kelly's self-awareness about her entertainment preferences is character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Let me assign you all TV network identities based on your personalities.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I'm MTV, VH1, E!, Bravo, and the Real Housewives channel.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's... not how this works.

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: I contain multitudes.

jokes.text: Wait, why are you smiling? / What's with the smile?
jokes.setup: Dwight giving earnest presentation to Gabe
jokes.punchline: Dwight noticing Gabe's inappropriate reaction mid-pitch
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment where Dwight realizes he's not being taken seriously

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: As you can see from this chart, our productivity metrics have increased significantly in the past quarter.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Gabe? Are you... is that a smile? I'm presenting quantitative data on departmental efficiency.

jokes.text: Put your hand on my hand. / Flush! / Ow!
jokes.setup: Gabe trying his toilet metaphor on Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's violent reaction to the condescending gesture
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Gabe's toilet metaphor with Dwight's characteristically physical response

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: A toilet is like a woman. You have to treat it with respect.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I don't need your metaphors, Gabe. I know how to treat women and toilets.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: Do you though?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Yes!

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: There, there.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Did you just pat me on the head like I'm a child?!

jokes.text: Listen, you're a perfectly fine toilet. I'm just an extraordinary piece of crap.
jokes.setup: Dwight forcing Gabe to help him after the hand incident
jokes.punchline: Dwight using Gabe's metaphor against him perfectly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that turns Gabe's metaphor inside out - peak Dwight cleverness with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I need your help, Gabe. I've injured my hand and I require assistance with my duties.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: Why should I help you? You're like a wounded animal.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Exactly. And you know what happens to wounded animals, Gabe? They either heal... or they get put down. I suggest you help me heal.

jokes.text: I'm sure you're just checking your Grindr account.
jokes.setup: Ryan using his phone during trivia
jokes.punchline: Host's casual assumption about gay dating app usage
jokes.explanation: Contextual humor for gay bar setting but not particularly original or character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Trivia Host
dialogue.line: Is that Grindr?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: What? No!

dialogue.speaker: Trivia Host
dialogue.line: It's cool, man. No judgment.

jokes.text: I can't not... touch it. / I can't not have my phone. I'm sorry. I want to be with my phone.
jokes.setup: Ryan being told to put away his phone
jokes.punchline: Ryan's pathetic phone addiction preventing him from competing
jokes.explanation: Ryan's millennial tech addiction taking priority over team goals

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I can't put my phone away. I'm in the middle of something really important.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: I'm refreshing my email every two seconds to see if anyone's texting me about... anything.

jokes.text: So, the best chance of hitting our mark is now in the hands, and brains, of Kevin, Meredith, Erin, and Kelly. Do I like these odds? My answer is no.
jokes.setup: A-team is eliminated, backup team is their only hope
jokes.punchline: Andy's brutal honesty about his employees' capabilities
jokes.explanation: Andy's desperate situation leading to unfortunate but honest assessment

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, look. The A-team is gone. So now we're down to our backup team. Which is basically just a bunch of people I wouldn't trust to water my plants.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I mean, Kevin can barely remember to show up to work, Erin thinks the copy machine is haunted, and don't even get me started on Ryan.

jokes.text: Oh, wait. Except for the Einsteins.
jokes.setup: Everyone gets Einstein question right
jokes.punchline: The team named 'Einsteins' got Einstein question wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - the team with the genius name failing the genius question

dialogue.speaker: Trivia Host
dialogue.line: Next question: What did Einstein discover?

dialogue.speaker: Trivia Host
dialogue.line: The team named 'Einsteins' got it wrong.

jokes.text: What? / I did my part, babe. I'm just the bell girl.
jokes.setup: Kevin hits bell without knowing answer
jokes.punchline: Erin's oblivious division of labor explanation
jokes.explanation: Erin's innocent misunderstanding of how trivia teamwork should function

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: What is the capital of France?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: *dings bell*

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Kevin, you have to know the answer before you ring in.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I thought that was your job.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: My job is to ask the questions and ring the bell when you get it right. Your job is to know the answers.

jokes.text: Trust me. One nine-minute bout is a cardiovascular equivalent of running uphill for three hours. I could go to this gym three times a week, or I can wrestle Stu once a month.
jokes.setup: Robert explaining his exercise routine to Dwight
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous math justifying minimal exercise through wrestling
jokes.explanation: Robert's pseudo-intellectual justification for bizarre lifestyle choices

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: I wrestle for 2 hours a day. That's 730 hours a year. Most people don't exercise at all, so I'm way ahead.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's not how math works.

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: Okay, well, I'm not a mathematician.

jokes.text: Dial it back, this isn't tail feathers, okay?
jokes.setup: Team celebrating trivia success
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate reference to gay club culture
jokes.explanation: Andy's awkward attempt to show gay bar knowledge falls flat and seems forced

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We crushed it! We are on fire! This calls for a celebration. We should go to a gay bar!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? I love gay bars. The music is great, the drinks are strong, and nobody's trying to get in my pants.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, that came out wrong.

jokes.text: A manager, a salesman, a leader, and a warrior walk into a restaurant. The hostess says, 'table for one?' How is this possible? / All those people are you?
jokes.setup: Dwight's riddle about his multiple roles
jokes.punchline: Robert immediately solving Dwight's self-aggrandizing riddle
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight grandiosity deflated by Robert's immediate understanding - well-structured riddle joke

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I am the fire that burns within the hearts of men. I am the steel that forges their will. I am assistant to the regional manager, and assistant regional manager. What am I?

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: You're a idiot.

jokes.text: Florida is America's basement... It's wet, it's filled with mold, strange insects, alligators.
jokes.setup: Robert explaining why Dwight shouldn't want the Florida job
jokes.punchline: Brutal but accurate description of Florida as basement
jokes.explanation: Robert's sophisticated disdain for Florida with memorably harsh metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Robert California
dialogue.line: You don't want to go to Florida, Dwight. Florida is just a penis attached to the rest of America. And like many penises, it does not have a brain.

jokes.text: Alligators are dinosaurs, Dwight. You know that, right? / N... it's complicated.
jokes.setup: Robert mentioning alligators as Florida problem
jokes.punchline: Dwight's uncertain response to simple evolutionary fact
jokes.explanation: Dwight's knowledge gaps exposed by Robert's casual scientific observation

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: You know, alligators are a real problem down in Florida.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I thought alligators evolved from crocodiles?

dialogue.speaker: Robert
dialogue.line: No, Dwight. Crocodiles evolved from alligators.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Oh. Okay.

jokes.text: It's a job interview, not a flea market.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering his grandfather's medal as tribute
jokes.punchline: Robert's dismissive response to inappropriate gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Robert line - cutting through Dwight's ceremony with practical observation

dialogue.speaker: Robert California
dialogue.line: Dwight, I don't want your grandfather's medal. What am I going to do with that? Wear it to my country club and tell everyone I won it for... what, exactly? Being a good grandson?

jokes.text: Marie Cotillard exposes herself a number of times in that film.
jokes.setup: Final trivia question about French art film
jokes.punchline: Meredith's reason for knowing obscure foreign cinema
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith - her unexpected cultural knowledge has predictably inappropriate motivation

dialogue.speaker: Meredith
dialogue.line: I've seen a lot of French films. Most of them were playing in the background at bars I was passed out in.

jokes.text: A fluke is one of the most common fish in the sea. So, if you go fishing for a fluke, chances are, you just might catch one.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to find wisdom in their trivia failure
jokes.punchline: Tortured fish metaphor that doesn't quite work as intended
jokes.explanation: Andy's attempt at motivational speaking through confused fish metaphor - character appropriate but execution weak

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You know, when you give a man a fish, he eats for a day. When you teach a man to fish, he eats for a lifetime. But when you torture the fish first... I don't know, maybe he loses his appetite?

jokes.text: First prize is $4,500 dollars! / Let's just do our thing, collect our hardware and get the hell home.
jokes.setup: Much bigger prize at championship level
jokes.punchline: Andy's overconfident assumption they'll win easily
jokes.explanation: Andy's typical overconfidence setting up for likely failure - predictable but in character

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: And now, the championship round, with a prize of $50,000!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Fifty thousand dollars? We're gonna be so rich. I'm already mentally spending it on a boat.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: A really nice boat. With a hot tub on it.

tmdb_overview: Pressed to boost the bottom line, Andy finds a novel way to improve sales, while Dwight explores corporate opportunities outside Scranton.
insight: Fifty jokes in 21 minutes—a dense episode that leans on character awkwardness over memorable punchlines.
